source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Creative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FLORA

We are building the first creative operating system: an infinite canvas designed for the generative computing paradigm. We’re a team of ~45 focused on elevating professional craft, backed by tier-one investors including Redpoint, Menlo Ventures, and a16z, as well as founders like Guillermo Rauch and Justin Kan. Our platform is already the choice of world-class creative powerhouses like Pentagram, Lionsgate, and Nike.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Creative teams everywhere are rethinking how they concept, design, and produce in a generative age—but many need a trusted partner to help bridge the potential of AI and the practical realities of the creative process.


As a Forward Deployed Creative, you’ll bridge creative potential and practical adoption. This is a hybrid role spanning roughly 70% Customer Success / Sales, 20% Marketing, and 10% Product. You’ll guide world-class creative teams to success, create content that inspires, and bring insights directly back into the product.




HOW YOU’LL MAKE AN IMPACT



Customer Success / Sales Enablement

 - Partner with Sales on calls to demonstrate FLORA’s creative and business value.

 - Design and lead workshops that teach teams how to integrate FLORA into their processes.

 - Build custom FLORA workflows tailored to customer’s creative briefs, projects, and use cases.

 - Support customer onboarding and help teams integrate FLORA into their creative processes.

Marketing

 - Create tutorials, case studies, and campaign content that showcase best practices, creative use cases, and new product launches.

 - Host livestreams, webinars, and events that inspire the community and demonstrate the power of FLORA in real creative contexts.

 - Develop workflows, apps, and techniques for FLORA’s community page and content library.

 - Engage actively across social channels and at events to represent FLORA’s brand and connect with creators worldwide.

Product

 - Synthesize customer feedback to help improve the product.

 - Work closely with engineering and design to close the loop between user insights and product improvements.

Partnerships

 - Develop and execute strategies to help FLORA reach and engage specific creative industries.

 - Build relationships with creative leaders and organizations, turning early advocates into long-term partners and champions.
   
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Domain Expertise & Versatility

You’re deeply grounded in a creative domain—whether design, marketing, fashion, film, or beyond. You understand the workflows, pain points, and language of that world, and can speak as a respected peer. At the same time, you’re versatile enough to jump into any customer context or industry and quickly show how FLORA can elevate their process.



FLORA & AI Expertise

You have hands-on experience with FLORA and AI tools—and more importantly, a genuine passion for them. You naturally experiment, share your discoveries, and can’t help but spread your excitement. You’re a practitioner at heart—someone who learns by doing and constantly pushes what’s possible with generative tools.



Customer-Facing Experience

You’re a natural communicator—personable, professional, and able to build strong relationships with creative teams. You can connect with customers across industries, understand their needs, and translate them into practical solutions. You love solving problems, teaching others, and enabling creative success. You’re comfortable being the face of FLORA in public settings—presenting on livestreams, at events, and in recorded tutorials.



Community

You’re an active member of the creative and AI ecosystem and bring both credibility and community with you. You know the conversations, studios, and emerging voices shaping the field, and you can represent FLORA authentically among them.



In addition, here are common traits that we look for across all our roles on the GTM team:

 - Passionate, purpose-led, and all-in. You are obsessed with working towards our v

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Applied AI
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FLORA

We are building the first creative operating system: an infinite canvas designed for the generative computing paradigm. We’re a team of ~45 focused on elevating professional craft, backed by tier-one investors including Redpoint, Menlo Ventures, and a16z, as well as founders like Guillermo Rauch and Justin Kan. Our platform is already the choice of world-class creative powerhouses like Pentagram, Lionsgate, and Nike.



We’re looking to bring on a Software Engineer who will architect and build the agentic systems powering FLORA.



As part of the founding team, you will work closely with the founders to invent how creative professionals interact with AI in a generative age. This is a first-principles role: you’ll design, implement, and scale the backbone of agentic workflows that let creatives orchestrate text, image, and video models seamlessly on FLORA’s infinite canvas.



What You’ll Do
As an Applied AI Engineer, you’ll design and build the intelligence layer of FLORA — systems that make multi-modal creative workflows powerful, reliable, and intuitive. You’ll work on problems at the intersection of AI, orchestration, and interaction design: bringing together text, image, and video models into seamless workflows that creatives can actually trust and enjoy using.



You’ll collaborate across product, infra, and design to build agentic features that let creatives speak their idea into existence while maintaining control.



Requirements

 - Hands-on experience building agentic systems — e.g. multi-step pipelines, tool-using agents, assistants with memory, or similar.

 - Familiarity with evaluation frameworks (automated checks, regression testing, human-in-the-loop scoring) to ensure system reliability.

 - Experience with memory and retrieval (vector DBs, embeddings, summarization, context management).

 - Strong background in orchestration across models, APIs, and tools — including handling retries, fallbacks, and edge cases.

 - Comfortable reasoning about multi-modal workflows (text, image, video) and their integration in a user-facing product.

 - Ability to work across the stack (frontend, backend, infra) and collaborate closely with product and design.

 - Product-minded — you care as much about usability and creative control as you do about technical novelty.

 - 3+ years of engineering experience

 - Bonus: experience with creative tools, generative models, or node-based interfaces.




WORKING AT FLORA




OFFICE POLICY

We work in-person at the Domino Refinery building in Williamsburg, Brooklyn—with views of the East River.




COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS

Competitive salary and meaningful equity. We also offer an Equinox membership and comprehensive health and dental coverage.

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Ecosystem
source_jobs.description:
About FLORA

We are building the first creative operating system: an infinite canvas designed for the generative computing paradigm. We’re a team of ~45 focused on elevating professional craft, backed by tier-one investors including Redpoint, Menlo Ventures, and a16z, as well as founders like Guillermo Rauch and Justin Kan. Our platform is already the choice of world-class creative powerhouses like Pentagram, Lionsgate, and Nike.



About the role

We're looking for a Senior Product Manager, Ecosystem to own the products that extend FLORA beyond the canvas—our creator marketplace, developer platform, and the features that unlock team and enterprise workflows.

You'll own products that turn FLORA from a tool into a platform: a marketplace where power users package and share their work, APIs and integrations that let developers embed FLORA into their own systems, and collaboration features that expand us from individual users to teams. These products sit at the intersection of creator economy, developer experience, and enterprise readiness.

You'll own the full roadmap for this surface area: defining marketplace mechanics, shaping creator and developer incentives, and building the governance systems that maintain quality at scale. You'll work closely with Engineering, Design, and Go-to-Market to ship features that turn individual users into teams and teams into advocates.

This is a senior role with significant ownership. You'll make hard calls on prioritization, define success metrics, and represent the collaboration vision to leadership and the company.

We're looking for people who have

 - 5+ years of product management experience, with at least 2 years on marketplace, platform, or API products

 - Experience bootstrapping a marketplace or creator economy—you've solved cold-start problems and know how to cultivate supply while generating demand

 - Familiarity with credit-based or usage-based pricing models—you've thought deeply about unit economics, consumption patterns, and incentive design

 - Experience with app stores, review processes, or content moderation systems—you understand the tradeoffs between openness and quality control

 - Technical depth—you can hold your own in conversations about API design, authentication, rate limiting, and developer experience

 - Strong product instincts and the ability to translate ambiguous opportunities into concrete roadmaps

 - Excellent cross-functional communication—you can align engineering, design, sales, and leadership around a shared vision

Exceptionally strong candidates might also have

 - Direct experience with MCP (Model Context Protocol) or similar agent/tool integration frameworks

 - Experience vibe coding — whether to build internal tooling or design/product prototypes

 - A background in AI-native products or creative tools

 - Experience scaling a techniques/templates/plugins marketplace (Figma, Notion, Zapier, etc.)

 - Familiarity with enterprise purchasing workflows—procurement, SSO, workspace governance

 - A track record shipping developer-facing products (APIs, SDKs, documentation, developer portals)

 - Experience at a company that transitioned from product to platform

Working at FLORA

Office policy We work in-person at the Domino Refinery building in Williamsburg, Brooklyn—with views of the East River.

Compensation and benefits Competitive salary and meaningful equity. We also offer an Equinox membership and comprehensive health and dental coverage.

source_jobs.title: Founding Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About FLORA

We are building the first creative operating system: an infinite canvas designed for the generative computing paradigm. We’re a team of ~45 focused on elevating professional craft, backed by tier-one investors including Redpoint, Menlo Ventures, and a16z, as well as founders like Guillermo Rauch and Justin Kan. Our platform is already the choice of world-class creative powerhouses like Pentagram, Lionsgate, and Nike.


About the role

We're hiring a Senior or Staff Data Engineer to build FLORA's data function from scratch. You'd be the first data hire, working directly with the CTO and Head of Product to define what data means here: how we capture it, store it, analyze it, and turn it into decisions.

You'll wire up our warehousing and pipelines, pin down the metrics that actually matter (activation moments, power users, engagement quality), and build dashboards that get used. Our PostHog telemetry is live. The broader data model is yours to design.

You'll work closely with our growth engineering team: you design the tracking model, they instrument against it. Vendor decisions are yours too, picking BI tooling, making the infrastructure calls that'll hold as we scale.

What you'll do

 - Audit existing PostHog data, events, and user behaviors

 - Design and implement data models with solid metadata collection

 - Stand up data infrastructure, warehousing, and processing pipelines

 - Evaluate and select core technologies

 - Build executive-level BI dashboards that drive product and growth decisions

We're looking for

 - 6+ years in data engineering or analytics engineering (former people managers returning to IC work are strongly encouraged)

 - Proven ability to work on your own; you're the first data hire and we're counting on your judgment

 - Deep SQL fluency and strong fundamentals in at least 1 modern data platform (Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, ClickHouse, MotherDuck etc.)

 - Hands-on infrastructure experience: you've set up pipelines, defined schemas, made tooling decisions from scratch

 - Strong product instincts and real business curiosity; you know what to measure, what's vanity, and how to frame findings for stakeholders

 - A hypothesis-driven approach; you ask questions about the business, not just the query

 - Comfort in hypergrowth; you can build fast and build for scale

 - Availability to work in-person 5 days a week

Nice to have

 - Python proficiency (preferred, though we're flexible on other languages)

 - TypeScript; we're a full-stack TS shop and you'll collaborate closely with engineers

 - Deep PostHog experience (cohorts, funnels, feature flags, warehouse sync)

 - Experience with activation models, conversion rate analysis, and A/B testing

 - Background in SaaS/web app analytics, or adjacent fields like supply chain or finance

 - Experience with creative tools or product-led growth companies

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer - Applied AI
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FLORA

We are building the first creative operating system: an infinite canvas designed for the generative computing paradigm. We’re a team of ~45 focused on elevating professional craft, backed by tier-one investors including Redpoint, Menlo Ventures, and a16z, as well as founders like Guillermo Rauch and Justin Kan. Our platform is already the choice of world-class creative powerhouses like Pentagram, Lionsgate, and Nike.

About the role

We're hiring a backend engineer to build and scale the infrastructure behind FLORA's multi-modal AI engine.


You'll work directly with the founders. The core problem: creatives run heavy, multi-step workflows on the canvas in real time, and the backend infra has to keep up. Your main job is a centralized execution engine that manages every model and tool we plug in.




What you'll do


Build the architecture that keeps the canvas fast and reliable as we scale to more generative models and tools.

 - Own and design the execution engine. One system that routes every generative tool, external model API, and canvas transformation through a standard path.

 - Speed up the canvas and cut data overhead.Cut database query overhead. Move tool configs and execution state into caching and in-memory stores so the canvas stays fast even when a workflow spans many nodes.

 - Handle failure well. Generative APIs time out, hit rate limits, and choke on big payloads. Build retries, circuit breakers, and fallbacks into the routing layer so one failed model doesn't kill the user's session.

 - Scale the multi-modal pipeline to millions of users. Work with product and applied AI to move, process, and store heavy payloads (text, high-res images, video) without bottlenecks.

Requirements

 - 3+ years on backend infrastructure, platform engineering, or distributed systems.

 - Hands-on experience building execution layers, execution graphs, state machines, or routing services.

 - A track record of finding database bottlenecks and fixing them: caching, query tuning, schema redesigns.

 - Comfort with multi-modal infrastructure: managing state and streaming for text, image, and video.

 - You care how backend latency and reliability show up in what the user can actually do on the canvas.

 - TypeScript familiarity and comfort in a serverless/edge runtime world

Bonus

 - Event-driven architectures, durable-workflow/state-machine engines, or graph execution engines.

 - Backends for node-based, real-time, or collaborative (CRDT/multiplayer) interfaces.

 - Familiarity with our stack - Convex, Liveblocks, Vercel Workflow, sandboxed code execution - or experience standing up an internal model/config registry.

notes: FLORA is a creative operating system with a strong focus on engineering and AI.
